jokes.text: Tell her it is a bit dangerous. - Not all Americans have guns. - Tell her Dave's got a paintball gun.
jokes.setup: Jemaine wants to make NYC sound dangerous to their mother
jokes.punchline: The best evidence of danger is Dave's paintball gun, which immediately gets undermined as 'not real'
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation structure where each attempt to sound tough gets immediately deflated

jokes.text: Tell her about all the TV stations here, Bret. - Jemaine wants me to tell you there's a lot of TV stations. - How many? - I don't know. There's a lot. Yeah yeah, no. More than four.
jokes.setup: Jemaine thinks having lots of TV stations is impressive enough to brag about
jokes.punchline: 'More than four' as if this is a meaningful measurement of abundance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and character-specific naivety about what constitutes impressive city features

jokes.text: Murray, I was wondering if you could call me by my rapping name? - Your rapping name? - Rhymenoceros. - Rhinoceros? - The Rhymenoceros.
jokes.setup: Bret wants to be taken seriously as a rapper
jokes.punchline: His 'cool' rap name is a clunky portmanteau that Murray immediately mishears
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Bret's earnest uncoolness, and becomes a running element

jokes.text: Nah, well, that's not gonna fit in here. I've got 'Bret.' Can't you just have Bret as your rapping name? - No. It's not a rapping name. - It's a good rapping name. 'Hello, everybody, I am Bret.'
jokes.setup: Murray's practical approach to the rap name problem
jokes.punchline: Murray earnestly demonstrates how 'Bret' could work as a rap introduction, completely missing the point of rap names
jokes.explanation: Murray's oblivious practicality creates perfect comedy through character consistency

jokes.text: Look, if you're gonna have some sort of rap name, just be careful you don't do a drive-by. - I won't. - Well, you'd better not.
jokes.setup: Murray giving practical advice about rap names
jokes.punchline: He earnestly warns against drive-by shootings as if it's a natural consequence of having a rap name
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic delivered with complete sincerity, perfect for Murray's character

jokes.text: What about you, Jemaine? Have you got a rapping name? - Yeah, 'Hiphopopotamus,' but you can just call me Jemaine if you want.
jokes.setup: Following Bret's elaborate rap name setup
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's equally ridiculous portmanteau delivered with casual indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect companion to Rhymenoceros, equally absurd but with Jemaine's characteristic nonchalance

jokes.text: I mean, you don't hear of professional musicians with long hair. - Well, Led Zeppelin? - I mean a man.
jokes.setup: Murray making a general statement about musicians and hair
jokes.punchline: His correction implies Led Zeppelin aren't men, revealing his narrow view of masculinity
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection that reveals Murray's backwards thinking in just four words

jokes.text: Most bands play at night. - It's too dangerous out there at night. - We go around walking around at night all the time. - Well, you know, anything could happen. You could get run over, pickpocketed, fall down a manhole, bump into people, murdered... imagine that... or even just ridiculed.
jokes.setup: Murray listing the dangers of being out at night
jokes.punchline: The escalation from practical dangers to murder, then deflating to 'ridiculed' as if it's equally serious
jokes.explanation: Great escalation structure where 'ridicule' is treated as seriously as murder by Murray

jokes.text: We've never been ridiculed. - No. - You haven't? - No. - Well, that's a surprise. I get ridiculed all the time.
jokes.setup: The guys claiming they've never been ridiculed
jokes.punchline: Murray's genuine surprise, oblivious to how ridiculous they clearly are
jokes.explanation: Murray's obliviousness to their obvious ridiculousness creates ironic humor

jokes.text: Oh hey, ginger-balls. - You know? - That was Bret. - He called you that.
jokes.setup: Murray giving an example of being ridiculed
jokes.punchline: Bret and Jemaine immediately reveal it was Bret who called him that
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Murray's victim narrative through awkward revelation

jokes.text: I thought that was your nickname? - No. I get, 'Oh, what are you on your way to... a dick meeting?'
jokes.setup: Murray trying to give another example of ridicule
jokes.punchline: The 'insult' is so childish and nonsensical it's more ridiculous than hurtful
jokes.explanation: The absurdity of the 'insult' makes Murray seem more pathetic than victimized

jokes.text: You should wear these T-shirts with 'New York' on them. All right, just so you fit in. Here's a hat. Put this on, all right? 'Hey, I'm walkin' here!'
jokes.setup: Murray giving advice on how to blend in as New Yorkers
jokes.punchline: His idea of blending in is the most touristy possible outfit plus a cliche movie quote
jokes.explanation: Murray's complete misunderstanding of how to blend in creates gentle mockery of tourist stereotypes

jokes.text: And these are the safety reflective belts. All right, they light up. - I'm not gonna wear that. - Yeah, you should. - No, I'm not wearing it.
jokes.setup: Murray's safety preparations escalating to absurd levels
jokes.punchline: Safety reflective belts as urban survival gear, with Jemaine's flat refusal
jokes.explanation: Visual absurdity of reflective belts for city walking, with character-appropriate resistance

jokes.text: Would you rather get murdered?
jokes.setup: Jemaine refusing the safety belt
jokes.punchline: Murray's earnest question as if these are the only two options
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character logic - Murray genuinely sees this as a reasonable either/or choice

jokes.text: You got a cigarette? - Sorry, we don't smoke. - Oh, that's too bad. Because my man Mickey here wants a cigarette, so now you're gonna have to give him some money.
jokes.setup: Seemingly innocent request for a cigarette
jokes.punchline: The illogical escalation from 'no cigarette' to 'give us money' delivered as if it makes perfect sense
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic of mugging presented as reasonable cause-and-effect

jokes.text: Listen, you don't want to mess with him, because he's crazy, man, all right? He's a psycho-killer. Qu'est-ce que c'est?
jokes.setup: Trying to make his partner sound threatening
jokes.punchline: Quoting Talking Heads lyrics ('Psycho Killer') as if it's genuine intimidation
jokes.explanation: Clever musical reference that works on multiple levels - both intimidating and ridiculous

jokes.text: Well, I've got $15. - Okay, hand it over. - No, but it's in the bank. We'd have to go to the bank to get it out. - No, we don't have time to go to the bank, dipshit. - We might just have time.
jokes.setup: Standard mugging scenario
jokes.punchline: Bret's literal-minded approach to the banking logistics and his helpful suggestion about timing
jokes.explanation: Bret's earnest helpfulness in his own mugging perfectly captures his character

jokes.text: Why... why does the phone have a camera glued to it? - It's a camera phone. Jemaine wanted one for his birthday. - Oh yeah? Where'd you get it? - I made it. It's homemade.
jokes.setup: Mugger examining their possessions
jokes.punchline: Bret's DIY approach to technology - literally gluing a camera to a phone
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag that establishes their technological ineptitude and Bret's earnest resourcefulness

jokes.text: Yeah, it's a piece of shit. - Well, how come Jemaine likes it so much? - You can have it. - No no, he doesn't want it.
jokes.setup: Mugger insulting the homemade phone
jokes.punchline: Bret defending Jemaine's appreciation for his gift, then immediately giving it away
jokes.explanation: Bret's conflicted loyalty between defending his gift and appeasing the mugger

jokes.text: You'd better be careful, because Bret knows karate. - Yeah, I got a book on karate. I haven't actually read it yet, but I've got...
jokes.setup: Jemaine trying to make Bret sound threatening
jokes.punchline: Bret's immediate deflation - he owns but hasn't read a karate book
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation where Bret immediately undermines any intimidation factor

jokes.text: Shut up, you English faggots, and give me the bike!
jokes.setup: Mugger getting frustrated
jokes.punchline: Calling New Zealanders 'English' - wrong country entirely
jokes.explanation: Geographic ignorance creates mild humor but relies on slur for impact

jokes.text: You'd better watch out. You don't really know who you're dealing with.
jokes.setup: Bret trying to sound intimidating before the rap battle
jokes.punchline: Sets up the absurd rap battle that follows
jokes.explanation: Effective setup for the musical number, building false confidence

jokes.text: I'm the mother-flippin' Rhymenoceros / My beats are phat and the birds are on my back and I'm horny
jokes.setup: Bret trying to intimidate through rap
jokes.punchline: Childish 'mother-flippin'' and confusing horny/horn imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Bret's earnest attempt at hardcore rap comes out adorably innocent

jokes.text: I was raised by a rapper and writer... they dated / And subsequently procreated
jokes.setup: Bret explaining his rap credentials
jokes.punchline: Overly formal 'subsequently procreated' as a rap lyric
jokes.explanation: Academic vocabulary in rap context creates perfect character-specific humor

jokes.text: Flows, they glow like phosphorus / Poppin' off the top of the esophagus
jokes.setup: Jemaine trying to create impressive rap metaphors
jokes.punchline: Overly scientific vocabulary that doesn't quite make sense as boasting
jokes.explanation: Scientific terminology in rap creates humor through incongruity and character specificity

jokes.text: I'm not a large water-dwelling mammal / Where did you get that preposterous hypothesis? / Did Steve tell you that, perchance?
jokes.setup: Jemaine addressing the hippopotamus connection
jokes.punchline: Academic language ('preposterous hypothesis') and blaming the random Steve
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about his own rap name using unnecessarily formal vocabulary

jokes.text: My rhymes and records, they don't get played / Because my records and rhymes, they don't get made / And if you rap like me, you don't get paid / And if you roll like me, you don't get laid
jokes.setup: Traditional rap boasting structure
jokes.punchline: Complete inversion - boasting about failure and inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of rap braggadocio - perfect structure turning boasts into anti-boasts

jokes.text: My rhymes are so potent that in this small segment / I made all the ladies in the area pregnant
jokes.setup: Mock-aggressive rap boasting
jokes.punchline: Absurd claim about impregnating women through rap delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Over-the-top absurdist boast that works through sheer commitment to ridiculous premise

jokes.text: Yes, sometimes my lyrics are sexist / But you lovely bitches now should know I'm trying to correct this
jokes.setup: Acknowledging problematic lyrics
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating the sexism he claims to be correcting
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction where the attempt to be woke immediately proves the opposite

jokes.text: Other rappers diss me / They say my rhymes are sissy / Why? / Why? / Why exactly? / What... why? / Be more constructive with your feedback / Please, why?
jokes.setup: Addressing criticism of their rap skills
jokes.punchline: Earnest request for constructive criticism mid-rap battle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - treating rap battle like a workshop critique session

jokes.text: Why? 'Cause I rap about reality / Like me and my grandma drinking a cup of tea / There ain't no party like my nana's tea party
jokes.setup: Defending his 'real' rap content
jokes.punchline: His idea of hardcore reality is tea with grandma
jokes.explanation: Perfect inversion of rap authenticity - grandmother tea parties as street cred

jokes.text: You guys dancing a little bit? - No.
jokes.setup: After the elaborate rap battle
jokes.punchline: Mugger's casual observation deflates the entire performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation - reduces their intimidating rap battle to awkward dancing

jokes.text: That's not a knife. - Yeah, that's a knife. - Oh, it is a knife.
jokes.setup: Bret challenging the weapon
jokes.punchline: His immediate reversal when corrected, showing complete lack of toughness
jokes.explanation: Bret's instant capitulation undermines any attempt at intimidation

jokes.text: Bret, my sleeve just... help me, my sleeve's just caught on the fence. - I'm too scared, man. - Bret, the corduroy's caught on the fence, Bret.
jokes.setup: Dramatic chase scene
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's escape thwarted by his corduroy sleeve getting caught on a fence
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy - dramatic escape ruined by fabric choice and Bret abandoning him

jokes.text: There was two of them. - Two? That's not a gang. - It was a two-man gang. - Two-man gang. - Can you have that, David? - Well that's a pretty small gang. I mean, technically, the smallest gang possible.
jokes.setup: Describing their attackers as a gang
jokes.punchline: Logical breakdown of what constitutes a gang, with Dave's technical precision
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the technical requirements for gang membership

jokes.text: You just left him there to fight them himself? - Well, I left him on the... he was on the fence. - Bret! - Well, I was... I was very scared.
jokes.setup: Murray's shock at Bret's abandonment
jokes.punchline: Bret's weak justification that he left Jemaine 'on the fence' literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Bret's pathetic attempt to justify abandoning his friend creates uncomfortable humor

jokes.text: He may be dead. - He maybe did what? - He may be dead. - I know, but what did he maybe do? - He may be dead. - Are you guys fucking with me? - What? - Not alive. You know... dead. - Oh. Oh.
jokes.setup: Murray trying to understand Bret's statement
jokes.punchline: Extended confusion over 'may be dead' vs 'maybe did' leading to Murray thinking they're messing with him
jokes.explanation: Sustained confusion gag that builds through miscommunication to genuine frustration

jokes.text: I can't believe that he just ran off like that when the cops came and left me there.
jokes.setup: Jemaine talking to his captors about being abandoned
jokes.punchline: Complaining to the criminals about Bret's behavior, treating them like therapists
jokes.explanation: Absurd situation where victim bonds with criminals over shared grievances

jokes.text: You... kill people? - Maybe. - Why, you ever kill somebody? - Not really. - No? - Yeah, me neither.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about murder
jokes.punchline: Both admitting they haven't really killed anyone in the most casual way possible
jokes.explanation: Casual discussion of murder becomes oddly relatable bonding moment

jokes.text: Killed a monkey once. - Mmm. Uh-huh. - Don't tell anybody about that, okay? 'Cause I'm not proud of it.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about violence
jokes.punchline: Random monkey murder confession treated as minor embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd confession that's both shocking and weirdly humanizing

jokes.text: Hey guys, remember that time he got his hand caught in that jar? - What a dick. I mean, who gets their hand caught in a jar? - That was... that was actually me. - That was Bret.
jokes.setup: Criminals mocking Jemaine's supposed incompetence
jokes.punchline: They're actually mocking Bret, who's sitting right there
jokes.explanation: Awkward misdirected insult creates cringe comedy through mistaken identity

jokes.text: Jemaine was the one who helped you out. And he helped you when you got your head stuck in the chair. - Remember? - Yeah. You were there for hours.
jokes.setup: Defending Jemaine's helpfulness
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Bret getting his head stuck in a chair for hours
jokes.explanation: Escalating catalog of Bret's mishaps builds picture of his general incompetence

jokes.text: You know, I never understood a fucking word that guy said. - He did say 'what' a lot.
jokes.setup: John's observation about Jemaine's communication
jokes.punchline: Bret's mild defense that he said 'what' a lot, confirming the confusion
jokes.explanation: Understated humor about Jemaine's perpetual confusion being his defining trait

jokes.text: So I got to untie the guy, right? I got to apologize. He's bleeding all over the place, I got to help him. It was very awkward shit. I was very upset about it.
jokes.setup: John telling a story about violence
jokes.punchline: Treating accidentally shooting someone as a social awkwardness problem
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through casual treatment of serious violence as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Yeah, one time we were supposed to go to the movies, and... uh, 'March of the Penguins.' And he... I had bought the tickets, and he didn't turn up. He texted me, said, 'I'm not coming.'
jokes.setup: Bret comparing his experience to John's kneecapping story
jokes.punchline: His 'equivalent' trauma is being stood up for a penguin documentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast comedy showing Bret's sheltered life compared to actual violence

jokes.text: Yeah, one time we were supposed to go to the movies, and... uh, 'March of the Penguins.' - Oh, I love that movie. - And, he... I had bought the tickets, and he didn't turn up. He texted me, said, 'I'm not coming.'
jokes.setup: Bret trying to relate to John's story
jokes.punchline: Comparing movie cancellation to kneecapping someone
jokes.explanation: Bret's inability to gauge appropriate comparisons creates awkward humor

jokes.text: Well, that's... that's not really the same thing, is it? I mean... I... I shot a guy, you know? - Yours is worse.
jokes.setup: John pointing out the scale difference
jokes.punchline: Bret's matter-of-fact acknowledgment that shooting is worse than movie cancellation
jokes.explanation: Bret's casual acknowledgment of the absurd comparison shows his social cluelessness

jokes.text: Kind of sounds like, 'What? What, what? Don't poot me in there weeth him. I'm innocent. I'm innocent'? - Yes, that's definitely him. - That's him. - It's a little bit more sort of South African.
jokes.setup: Police officer describing the suspect
jokes.punchline: Terrible attempt at New Zealand accent that sounds more South African
jokes.explanation: Accent confusion humor with Bret's polite correction of the officer's attempt

jokes.text: 'Cause I rented your half of the apartment out to Rueben? - Does he have to stay here still? - Well, he's paid two weeks' rent, 'cause I didn't think you were coming back.
jokes.setup: Jemaine discovering Bret rented out his space
jokes.punchline: Bret's casual assumption that Jemaine was dead and practical response
jokes.explanation: Bret's practical approach to his friend's disappearance shows his emotional detachment

jokes.text: I wouldn't have done it if I'd known you were gonna hassle me about it. - You shouldn't have done it anyway. - Hassling me. - I'm not hassling you. - You are hassling me.
jokes.setup: Jemaine confronting Bret about abandonment
jokes.punchline: Bret treating justified anger as mere 'hassling'
jokes.explanation: Bret's complete inability to take responsibility creates cringe through deflection

jokes.text: Okay, David Hassle-hoff.
jokes.setup: Jemaine accusing Bret of hassling him
jokes.punchline: Weak pun on David Hasselhoff's name instead of addressing the issue
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that perfectly captures Bret's avoidance of serious issues

jokes.text: Did the cops try to strip search you? - No. - Did they find anything? - No, they didn't strip search me. - Oh, I... I thought you said they did. - No.
jokes.setup: Murray asking about Jemaine's time in jail
jokes.punchline: Murray's confusion about whether strip search happened shows his weird priorities
jokes.explanation: Murray's inappropriate curiosity about strip searches reveals his odd fascinations

jokes.text: Well, did any of your cellmates, you know, rape you in the a... - No. No no no. - Oh good, good. 'Cause if... if I was a convict, you know, and I was in a cell with a pretty boy like you, I would definitely wait till lights out, put my hand over your mouth...
jokes.setup: Murray checking on Jemaine's jail experience
jokes.punchline: Murray's inappropriate detailed description of prison assault scenarios
jokes.explanation: Murray's inappropriate prison fantasy creates maximum cringe through social awkwardness

jokes.text: Being in jail just makes you seem so sexy. I guess it's the danger aspect. What are you gonna do to me? Do it! No, don't, don't.
jokes.setup: Murray finding Jemaine's jail time attractive
jokes.punchline: Murray's conflicted sexual fantasy about dangerous Jemaine
jokes.explanation: Murray's inappropriate sexual fascination with 'dangerous' Jemaine creates deep cringe

jokes.text: It's like Bonnie and Clyde, you know. They would rob banks, and then they would do each other.
jokes.setup: Murray explaining his criminal attraction theory
jokes.punchline: Crude misunderstanding of the Bonnie and Clyde dynamic
jokes.explanation: Murray's simplistic view of crime/sex connection shows his juvenile understanding

jokes.text: That's not a real gangster name. - What? - And Murray. - Well, I've been on the inside, I know what sort of names gangsters have. Like John. There was another guy there... Ralph.
jokes.setup: Murray dismissing 'Rhymenoceros' as not gangster enough
jokes.punchline: His examples of 'real' gangster names are the most ordinary names possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation where Murray's 'street knowledge' leads to the most vanilla names

jokes.text: Um, Rhymenoceros. Rhymenoceros, yep. That's not a real gangster name. What? And Murray. Well, I've been on the inside, I know what sort of names gangsters have. Like John. There was another guy there... Ralph.
jokes.setup: Jemaine claiming expertise about gangster names from jail
jokes.punchline: His examples are the most mundane names possible - John and Ralph
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of gangster mystique with the most ordinary names possible

jokes.text: Well, you were only in there for two days, man.
jokes.setup: Murray acting like a hardened criminal
jokes.punchline: Bret's casual reminder of the minimal jail time
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Murray's prison expertise with simple factual correction

jokes.text: Sorry, if I can't trust him on the streets, I can't trust him on the stage. I can't do another gig with him without going against everything I stand for. - Well, what if you actually had another gig? - Well, then I would.
jokes.setup: Jemaine's principled stand about not performing with Bret
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning principles when reminded they don't have gigs anyway
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of moral posturing through practical reality check

jokes.text: But to be fair to Bret, John, you were one of those hoodlums mugging us, you know? - Yeah. - Yeah, that's true. I guess I am somewhat to blame as well, huh?
jokes.setup: John criticizing Bret's abandonment
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's polite reminder that John was literally the mugger, with John's casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd logic where the criminal casually accepts partial responsibility for his own crime

jokes.text: At least I didn't kill a monkey. - Dawg.
jokes.setup: Bret defending himself against abandonment charges
jokes.punchline: Bringing up John's monkey murder confession as his defense
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Bret's terrible moral reasoning and John's single-word defeat

jokes.text: Brought you a cup of tea, man. - No, thank you, Bret. - Please grab it, it's hot. - It's hot. - Deal with it yourself. That's what I've learned to do the hard way. Oh, ow ow ow... oh, fuck! Ow.
jokes.setup: Bret's peace offering of tea
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's rejection leads to Bret burning himself, with ironic comment about dealing with things
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with ironic timing - Bret's tough guy speech immediately undermined

jokes.text: Hey, Jemaine, I'll see you later, maybe. - Bye, Bret. - If I live, that is, 'cause I might get killed. - Okay, let me know how that goes.
jokes.setup: Bret's dramatic exit to face danger
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's casual response to potential death as if it's a minor update
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that treats death as casual inconvenience to report back on

jokes.text: Yeah, I didn't really love that camera phone as much as you thought I loved it. In fact, I kind of think you ruined my phone when you made me that camera phone. And my camera.
jokes.setup: Bret risking his life to get the camera phone back
jokes.punchline: Jemaine revealing he actually hated the gift and it ruined two devices
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing revelation that makes Bret's heroic gesture completely pointless

jokes.text: I didn't really love that camera phone as much as you thought I loved it. In fact, I kind of think you ruined my phone when you made me that camera phone. And my camera.
jokes.setup: Bret preparing heroic mission to retrieve camera phone
jokes.punchline: Jemaine reveals he actually hated the homemade camera phone
jokes.explanation: Good deflation revealing Bret's gesture is based on complete misunderstanding of what Jemaine wanted

jokes.text: There's children on the street using guns and knives / Taking drugs and each other's lives / Killing each other with knives and forks / Calling each other names like dork
jokes.setup: Serious social commentary song about urban violence
jokes.punchline: Escalation from guns and drugs to name-calling like 'dork'
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition where serious social issues get deflated to playground insults

jokes.text: # Who's touching these monkeys? # # Please leave these poor sick monkeys alone #
jokes.setup: Monkey disease problem
jokes.punchline: Earnest plea for monkey welfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bret's misguided social activism

jokes.text: There's people on the street getting diseases from monkeys / Yeah, that's what I said, they're getting diseases from monkeys / Now there's junkies with monkey disease / Who's touching these monkeys?
jokes.setup: Social commentary about urban problems
jokes.punchline: Bizarre focus on monkey disease transmission as major urban issue
jokes.explanation: Absurdist urban concern that reveals Bret's skewed priorities and logic

jokes.text: Why are we still paying so much for sneakers when they're made by little slave kids? What are your overheads?
jokes.setup: Continuing the child labor concern
jokes.punchline: Treating child exploitation as a business efficiency question
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bret's business-minded approach to moral outrage

jokes.text: Saw a man lying on the street half-dead with knives and forks sticking out of his leg / He said, 'Ah ah ah, ow ow ow ow ow ow / Can somebody get the knife and fork out of my leg please?' / 'Can somebody please remove these cutleries from my knees?'
jokes.setup: Dramatic urban violence scene
jokes.punchline: Polite request for cutlery removal and incorrect plural 'cutleries'
jokes.explanation: Absurd politeness in violent situation plus grammatical error creates character-specific humor

jokes.text: Here are the photos... I got 'em developed. From the camera phone. - You guys had some really good times, huh? - I hope you don't mind, there's some of me in there. I had to finish out the roll.
jokes.setup: Returning stolen camera phone
jokes.punchline: Mugger politely developed their photos and added his own to finish the roll
jokes.explanation: Absurdly considerate criminal behavior - treating theft like borrowing and being helpful

jokes.text: I just wish John was big enough to accept my apology, you know? - Well man, we're gonna get some pizza. Do you want to come and get a slice of pizza or something? - Yeah? Really? Oh, I'd love to, man.
jokes.setup: Mickey lamenting his broken friendship
jokes.punchline: Immediate enthusiasm for pizza with his former victims
jokes.explanation: Quick emotional pivot from sadness to excitement shows Mickey's simple priorities

jokes.text: Um, I'm going to a White Supremacist gig. It's gonna be awesome. - Oh yeah? Well, we'll probably just get the pizza.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about evening plans
jokes.punchline: Mickey's casual mention of attending hate group event, Bret's mild response
jokes.explanation: Dark humor through casual treatment of extremism as social event

jokes.text: Man, this whole week's been terrible. I haven't felt this bad since Cheeky died. Man, I miss that monkey.
jokes.setup: John expressing emotional pain
jokes.punchline: Revealing that 'Cheeky' was the monkey he killed earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy callback - recontextualizing murder confession as accidental pet killing
